name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: A 5th-century rock fortress and palace ruin rising 200m above the surrounding plain, famed for its mirror wall, frescoes, and lion-paw gateway. UNESCO World Heritage Site.
recognitionTag: ancient rock fortress with lion paw entrance

name: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
description: A Buddhist temple in the royal palace complex of Kandy, home to the relic of the tooth of the Buddha, one of the most sacred sites in the Buddhist world.
recognitionTag: ornate golden roofed temple by a lake

name: Galle Fort
description: A fortified city built by the Portuguese and expanded by the Dutch in the 17th century, with cobbled streets, colonial buildings, and ocean-facing ramparts.
recognitionTag: coastal stone fort walls with lighthouse

name: Nine Arches Bridge
description: A century-old railway viaduct built entirely of stone, brick, and cement without steel, set among tea plantations in the hill country.
recognitionTag: stone railway bridge with multiple arches in green hills

name: Dambulla Cave Temple
description: A cave monastery complex with over 150 Buddha statues and ancient murals spread across five caves, dating back over 2,000 years.
recognitionTag: cave temple entrance with buddha statues

name: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage
description: A sanctuary caring for orphaned and injured wild elephants, known for the daily river-bathing procession of its resident herd.
recognitionTag: herd of elephants bathing in a river

name: Anuradhapura Sacred City
description: The first ancient capital of Sri Lanka, home to well-preserved dagobas, monastery ruins, and the sacred Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi tree.

name: Polonnaruwa Ancient City
description: Sri Lanka's second ancient capital, featuring the Gal Vihara rock-cut Buddha statues and extensive royal palace ruins.
recognitionTag: rock cut reclining buddha statue ruins

name: Yala National Park
description: Sri Lanka's most visited national park, with one of the highest leopard densities in the world alongside elephants and abundant birdlife.
recognitionTag: leopard in dry scrub jungle safari park

name: Mirissa Beach
description: A crescent-shaped beach on the south coast known for whale watching, surfing, and a relaxed backpacker atmosphere.
recognitionTag: palm fringed tropical beach with fishing boats

name: Nuwara Eliya Tea Estates
description: Rolling hill-country tea plantations surrounding a cool-climate colonial-era town, with working tea factories open for tours.
recognitionTag: green tea plantation hills with pickers

name: Jaffna Fort
description: A star-shaped fort originally built by the Portuguese and later expanded by the Dutch, overlooking Jaffna lagoon in the north.
